title: Haloragis teucrioides
intro: Haloragis teucrioides, also known as New Zealand buttons or water-dragon, is a small, annual or perennial plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Haloragis teucrioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly prepared in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Haloragis teucrioides is a species of plant that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. It is a native Australian plant, commonly known as the "teucrioid watermilfoil" or "water-dilly-grass". Haloragis teucrioides is a unique species with distinctive characteristics, including its milky sap and small, green or yellow flowers.
